title: aster asperellus
intro: Aster asperellus, also known as alpine aster, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and inflammation due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Aster asperellus include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain, promoting healing, and preventing infection. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, and can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
